reasoning: Historically, the Treasury typically targets a TGA balance of $750k-$850k at end-of-quarter or outside of debt-limit periods. The current level of ~$905k in late October 2025 is very high and above this target band. Statistical mean-reversion suggests that such elevated balances are likely to drain back down to normal operating levels in the immediate short term. The FRED statistical tool reflects this mean-reversion, estimating a P(increase) of 0.33. November typically sees a seasonal cash drain (for instance, dropping to ~$737k by mid-November 2024), further reducing the chance of an increase in the November dates. January does see a build-up around the mid-month estimated tax deadline, though it usually maxes out around the $800k range. Following the mandatory rule that uncertainty increases over time, the probabilities are monotonically moved closer to 0.5 for the more distant dates, reflecting increased chances of unanticipated policy shifts, target adjustments, or timing of debt issuance.
